name: auth
description: Full authentication (better-auth): email/password, sessions, and — via config — OAuth and 2FA. Installs a global guard so the API is secure by default; health and /api/auth stay public.
env: # auth (better-auth) — set a strong secret (>= 32 chars) in production
# 2FA (TOTP): set true to enable the plugin
# Reject breached passwords via Have I Been Pwned (calls the public range API)
# Email: SMTP for password reset + verification. Local dev targets Mailpit
# Require new sign-ups to verify their email before signing in
# OAuth (optional) — set a provider's id+secret to enable it
instructions: Create the auth tables:  npx @better-auth/cli migrate -y --config apps/api/src/auth/auth.ts
Sign up:  curl -c cookies.txt -XPOST localhost:3000/api/auth/sign-up/email -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"email":"a@example.com","password":"password123","name":"A"}'
Call a protected route with the session:  curl -b cookies.txt localhost:3000/account/me
Everything is now protected by default; mark public routes with @Public(). Enable OAuth by setting a provider's env vars; enable 2FA with AUTH_TWO_FACTOR=true.
